title: P1.6：72 小時逐 3 小時預報圖（比照 CWA 官網雙溫度曲線）
gate: GATE SATISFIED 2026-07-04: depends_on card was accepted (approved) with buffer before the 2026-07-06 12:00 deadline. Cleared for execution by management.
operational_notes: Live-mode town codes are now CWA-sourced (e.g. cwa-65000260 for 貢寮區), NOT the old starter slugs like newtaipei-gongliao. For any live testing, read codes from the live /api/towns response first.
The forecast payload now also carries sunrise/sunset and UV fields added by the previous card; do not regress them when extending the response with hourly slices.
Backend now validates date within today..today+6; the 72h chart gating must stay consistent with that window.

context.background: User reviewed the CWA official website's 72-hour forecast chart (dual temperature curves + weather icons per time slot) and asked for the same view. Investigation confirmed the backend CWA adapter already fetches the 3-hourly township dataset (F-D0047-093 family) for near-term dates, but normalize_to_daily collapses slices into daily highs/lows and discards the intra-day sequence. This card stops discarding that granularity and renders it.
context.honesty_note: The public opendata township feed is 3-hourly; the official website chart is 1-hourly (internal data). We ship the same chart style at 3-hour resolution and must not claim hourly precision.
context.value: For trips happening tomorrow / day after / +3 days, an intra-day curve (morning chill, midday peak, evening rain window) is more actionable than a single daily high/low pair.

in_scope: S1 backend: when the requested window overlaps the next 72h, include the raw-but-normalized 3-hourly slices in the forecast response (new field, e.g. hourly: [{time, temp_c, apparent_temp_c, pop_percent, weather, weather_code}]) sourced from the 3-hourly dataset; keep the existing daily summary untouched; mock mode returns deterministic mock slices
S2 backend: extend the TimeSlice normalization to carry apparent temperature (AT) and weather code alongside existing fields, with tolerant parsing (2026-07-06 CWA format change applies here too)

S3 frontend: a 72h chart on the result view — temperature and apparent-temperature curves, weather icon row (map CWA weather codes to a small emoji/icon set), 3-hour tick x-axis with day boundaries marked, PoP shown per slot (bar or label)
S4 frontend: chart implementation must be dependency-light (inline SVG or a tiny chart util); do not add a heavy charting library to the bundle
S5 chart only renders when the selected/travel window is within the next 72h; otherwise the daily cards remain the sole view
S6 tests: normalization of 3-hourly payload (mock-based), 72h window gating logic
out_of_scope: 1-hourly resolution or scraping the CWA website
一週溫度曲線 / 體感曲線 / 過去24小時 tabs from the official site
Any AI/Gemini work, AQI, weather warnings
Backend architecture changes beyond the response extension described

requirements: R1: Mock mode remains fully functional end-to-end with deterministic 3-hourly mock slices
R2: Tolerant parsing for all new fields; missing AT or weather code degrades gracefully (curve omitted or icon blank), never a crash
R3: UI labels the chart truthfully as 逐3小時 (no hourly claim)
acceptance_criteria: AC1: ruff + pytest pass on Ubuntu including new tests
AC2: npm run build passes; no new heavy chart dependency in package.json
AC3: Live mode: selecting a town with travel date = tomorrow renders the 72h chart with both temperature curves, icons, and PoP from non-mock data
AC4: Travel date beyond 72h: chart is absent, daily cards unaffected
AC5: Mock mode renders the chart from deterministic mock slices
AC6: Changes committed and pushed to origin

report_template.location: Ubuntu agent-bridge reports path or repo-local equivalent used by the current workflow

user_one_liner: After the UX-refresh card is accepted and if time allows before the 7/6 deadline, add a CWA-website-style 72h chart: expose the already-fetched 3-hourly slices from the backend and render dual temperature curves with weather icons, honestly labeled as 3-hourly resolution.
next_task_hint: If accepted with time remaining, revisit deferred items in order: Gemini activation with honest AI labeling, then MOENV AQI.
